---
title: "AI R Tutorials"
subtitle: "Learn R first — then you can trust (and fix) what AI writes."
sidebar: ai-curriculum
listing:
  contents:
    - cs-crash-course/index.qmd
  type: default
  sort: title asc
  fields: [title, description]
toc: false
format:
  html:
    page-layout: article
---

## Functional programming

::: {.panel-tabset}

### Beginner · Base R

Vectors, `if`/`else`, loops, `apply`, `lapply`. [Open →](level-1-base-r/index.qmd)

### Intermediate · dplyr

`mutate`, `filter`, `group_by`, `lead`/`lag`, joins. [Open →](level-2-dplyr/index.qmd)

### Intermediate · purrr & furrr

`map`, `safely`, `walk`, optional `furrr`. [Open →](level-3-purrr/index.qmd)

### Advanced · data.table

`[i, j, by]`, `.SD`, `shift`, `fcase`. [Open →](level-4-data-table/index.qmd)

:::

## Computer science crash course

Types, memory, debugging, reproducibility — light R. [Open →](cs-crash-course/index.qmd)

Use the **Coding curriculum** sidebar for every quest, or open a track above.

**Human R Tutorials** = full field workflows. **AI R Tutorials** = skill drills.

## Open teaching we build on

Quest pages end with **Go deeper (open access)**: paraphrased pointers to free courses and code from [Software Carpentry](https://swcarpentry.github.io/), [R for Data Science](https://r4ds.hadley.nz/), [Chris Brown / Seascape Models](https://www.seascapemodels.org/), St Andrews statistical ecology, UQ R workshops, [AniMove](https://animove.org/), Plymouth marine training, FIU open stats materials, and Galápagos community R courses—plus package docs for dplyr, purrr, and data.table. Functional programming tracks share one bibliography; the [CS crash course](cs-crash-course/index.qmd#go-deeper-open-access) adds reproducibility and workflow sources.
